description: FOSS tools for 6529 community members pointing agents at 6529.io. Self-hosted, privacy-preserving, agent-native. No API keys, no rate limits, no vendor lock-in.
philosophy: The 6529 ethos is self-sovereignty. These tools let you build your own agent stack without depending on proprietary services. Each tool replaces a paid/tracked alternative with a self-hosted equivalent that an agent can interact with programmatically.

tools.name: SearXNG
tools.what: Self-hosted meta-search engine aggregating results from 70+ sources
tools.why_for_6529: Agents researching 6529 topics need search without API rate limits or tracking
tools.agent_integration: HTTP API — agents query via curl/requests, get JSON results
tools.replaces: Google Search API, Bing Search API, SerpAPI

tools.name: Crawl4AI
tools.what: Open-source web crawler optimized for LLMs. Converts any webpage to clean markdown for agent ingestion
tools.why_for_6529: Extract 6529.io pages as clean markdown for your agent to read and analyze
tools.agent_integration: Python library or HTTP API — agents pass a URL, get clean markdown back
tools.replaces: Firecrawl, Jina Reader, proprietary scraping APIs

tools.name: Meilisearch
tools.what: Open-source full-text search engine. Lightning-fast typo-tolerant search
tools.why_for_6529: Index 6529.io content locally for fast agent queries across meme cards, profiles, waves, and network data
tools.agent_integration: REST API — agents search via HTTP POST, get JSON results

tools.name: Gitea
tools.what: Self-hosted Git. Open source, lightweight, API-driven
tools.why_for_6529: Agents can read/write repos programmatically. Host your own 6529-related tools, forks, or contributions
tools.agent_integration: REST API — full CRUD for repos, issues, PRs, releases

tools.name: Ghost
tools.what: Open-source publishing platform. SQLite backend, agent-readable database
tools.why_for_6529: Publish 6529 analysis, blog posts, or community content. Agents can write directly to the database
tools.agent_integration: SQLite DB direct access or Admin REST API — agents can read/write posts

tools.name: Quartz
tools.what: Static site generator from Obsidian markdown. Agent writes markdown, Quartz renders to HTML
tools.why_for_6529: Publish 6529 research notes or knowledge bases. Agents write Obsidian-compatible markdown, Quartz handles rendering
tools.agent_integration: Filesystem — agents write .md files, Quartz builds static HTML

tools.name: faster-whisper
tools.what: FOSS speech-to-text. Local, private, no API costs. Medium model runs on CPU
tools.why_for_6529: Transcribe 6529 community audio (spaces, AMAs, podcasts) for agent analysis. Also used for the OM Album karaoke lyrics pipeline
tools.agent_integration: Python library — agents call transcribe(), get word-level timestamps
tools.replaces: OpenAI Whisper API, Google Speech-to-Text, AWS Transcribe

tools.name: Ollama
tools.what: Run LLMs locally. Open source, no API costs. Agents can use local models for inference
tools.why_for_6529: Run a local LLM to analyze 6529 data without sending it to external APIs. Privacy-preserving for sensitive wallet analysis
tools.agent_integration: REST API — agents send prompts, get completions. Compatible with OpenAI API format
tools.replaces: OpenAI API, Anthropic API, Google Gemini API (for local inference)

tools.name: Cloudflare Tunnel
tools.what: Free, open tunneling. Expose local services to the internet without opening ports
tools.why_for_6529: Share your 6529 analysis dashboard, search index, or agent tools with the community without exposing your home network
tools.agent_integration: CLI — agents can configure tunnels programmatically via config files

tools.name: Tailscale
tools.what: WireGuard-based mesh VPN. Private network between machines
tools.why_for_6529: Connect your agent's machine to your data sources privately. Agents can reach services across machines as if local
tools.agent_integration: CLI/systemd — transparent network access, agents don't need special integration
tools.replaces: Traditional VPN, SSH tunneling, manual network configuration

tools.name: Docker
tools.what: Container runtime. Isolated services, reproducible environments
tools.why_for_6529: Run SearXNG, Meilisearch, Ghost, Gitea all in isolated containers on one machine. Reproducible 6529 research environments
tools.agent_integration: CLI — agents can start/stop/manage containers via docker command
tools.replaces: Manual service management, virtual machines, bare-metal deployment

tools.name: Hermes Agent
tools.what: Open-source autonomous agent framework. CLI-first, MCP-native, skills system, cron scheduling
tools.why_for_6529: Run an agent that can fetch 6529.io data, analyze meme cards, track TDH, and publish findings. The agent-native files in this PR are designed for agents like Hermes
tools.agent_integration: CLI — the agent IS the integration. It fetches files, processes data, and takes actions
tools.replaces: Manual API scripting, custom agent frameworks, proprietary AI assistants
tools.alternatives: OpenClaw — another open-source stateful agent system with different architecture

tools.name: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
tools.what: Open protocol for connecting AI agents to tools and data sources. Standardized, vendor-neutral
tools.why_for_6529: The 6529 MCP server exists — it exposes TDH, identities, ecosystem data, wave scores, and card details via standardized tools that any MCP-compatible agent can use
tools.agent_integration: Protocol — agents connect via MCP, get structured tool results. No custom API integration needed
tools.replaces: Custom API wrappers, proprietary tool integrations, manual data fetching

tools.name: OpenClaw
tools.what: Open-source stateful agent system with persistent memory and tool integration
tools.why_for_6529: Alternative to Hermes Agent for running agents that interact with 6529.io data. Different architecture, same goal: agent-native interaction with the 6529 ecosystem
tools.agent_integration: CLI — agents connect to tools via integrations, process data, and take actions
tools.replaces: Manual API scripting, custom agent frameworks, proprietary AI assistants
